summary: A larger volume at the back part of a lower front tooth, relative to its width near the biting surface, indicates it likely belongs to a mandibular lateral incisor. This specific dental structure distinguishes these from central or canine molars based on detailed examination techniques such as dissection planes. To be precise for an exam context, \boxed{\text{Lateral Incisor}} would fit here perfectly according to provided options.
